description: This is an automatically generated playbook based on the question: A detections playbook for T1531 of MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise framework is required.The target platform is Windows. You can find more information at https://attack.mitre.org/detectionstrategies/DET0120#AN0334 . The description of the approach follows: Correlated user account modification (reset, disable, deletion) events with anomalous process lineage (e.g., PowerShell or net.exe from an interactive session), especially outside of IT admin change windows or by non-admin users.

external_references.name: MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1531: Account Access Removal

items.content: Identify and collect Windows event logs related to user account modifications (Event IDs 4722, 4725, 4726) from domain controllers and member servers.
Collect process creation events (Event ID 4688) with command line arguments from endpoints, focusing on interactive user sessions.
Correlate account modification events with process creation events by user, timestamp, and source IP/hostname.
Check if account modifications occurred outside of established IT admin change windows.
Verify if the modifying user lacks administrative privileges (non-admin users performing modifications).
Inspect process lineage for use of suspicious tools (e.g., PowerShell, net.exe, net1.exe) in interactive sessions.
Investigate any anomalies by reviewing full command lines, parent processes, and network connections associated with the events.
If malicious activity is confirmed, isolate affected systems, reset compromised accounts, and revoke unauthorized access.
